techniques.name: Credentials from Password Stores
techniques.what_it_is: Stealing passwords saved in web browsers, password managers, and the OS keychain.
techniques.why_this_finding: Infostealer malware that ran on the victim's machine dumped every password the browser had saved at once — not one site's password, the whole store.

techniques.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
techniques.what_it_is: Stealing a browser's active session cookies to hijack a logged-in session without the password or a 2FA prompt.
techniques.why_this_finding: The same malware exfiltrated live session cookies, so an attacker can resume an authenticated session even with 2FA enabled.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.what_it_is: Logging in with legitimate stolen credentials instead of breaking in — the activity looks normal and is hard to detect.
techniques.why_this_finding: With the stolen passwords and cookies, an attacker signs in to the victim's real accounts directly; there is nothing to 'exploit' and little to flag.
